text.title: Task Creation Template Guide Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Task Creation Template Guide. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Author fills front-matter before body; missing front-matter aborts the fill flow.
rule.rationale: Body content depends on component + dependencies declared in front-matter.

rule.statement: component field is one of the entries in the impl-plan-components map.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc component names produce orphan graph edges.

rule.statement: Acceptance section uses the AC rubric (Given/When/Then or rubric items).
rule.rationale: Inconsistent AC formats produce ambiguous done.

rule.statement: Steps section is a numbered list (1., 2., 3., …); bullets rejected.
rule.rationale: Numbered steps imply order; bullets do not.

rule.statement: Notes section header always present; body may be empty.
rule.rationale: Section presence makes downstream parsing deterministic.

rule.statement: Author runs task-creation-principles rubric after fill; queue blocked until rubric green.
rule.rationale: Post-fill rubric catches violations before executor sees the TASK.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-task-creation-template-guide.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
